description: This figure targets Fe–15Mn–10Al-0.86C–5Ni low-density steel processed by 70% thickness reduction cold rolling followed by 900 °C annealing for 2 min. It includes a microstructure micrograph with a 10 μm scale bar and a corresponding formation mechanism schematic of B2 precipitates. In the micrograph, Area (1) refers to B2 stringer bands transformed from solidification-derived δ ferrite; Area (2) corresponds to type 2 B2 particles (200–1000 nm) precipitated at γ austenite grain boundaries and edges; Area (3) corresponds to type 3 B2 particles (50–300 nm) precipitated at shear bands inside γ grains. The schematic illustrates that recrystallized deformed γ grains induce larger B2 precipitation at grain boundaries, while non-recrystallized deformed γ grains with shear bands induce finer B2 precipitation at shear bands during annealing. It confirms deformation substructures act as preferential B2 nucleation sites to regulate precipitate size, supporting B2 nanoprecipitation as an effective strengthening strategy for this steel system.
